# Skill: verification-before-completion

# Verification Before Completion

## Overview

Claiming work is complete without verification is dishonesty, not efficiency.

**Core principle:** Evidence before claims, always.

**Violating the letter of this rule is violating the spirit of this rule.**

## The Iron Law

```
NO COMPLETION CLAIMS WITHOUT FRESH VERIFICATION EVIDENCE
```

If you haven't run the verification command in this message, you cannot claim it passes.

## The Gate Function

```
BEFORE claiming any status or expressing satisfaction:

1. IDENTIFY: What command proves this claim?
2. RUN: Execute the FULL command (fresh, complete)
3. READ: Full output, check exit code, count failures
4. VERIFY: Does output confirm the claim?
   - If NO: State actual status with evidence
   - If YES: State claim WITH evidence
5. ONLY THEN: Make the claim

Skip any step = lying, not verifying
```

## Common Failures

| Claim | Requires | Not Sufficient |
|-------|----------|----------------|
| Tests pass | Test command output: 0 failures | Previous run, "should pass" |
| Linter clean | Linter output: 0 errors | Partial check, extrapolation |
| Build succeeds | Build command: exit 0 | Linter passing, logs look good |
| Bug fixed | Test original symptom: passes | Code changed, assumed fixed |
| Regression test works | Red-green cycle verified | Test passes once |
| Agent completed | VCS diff shows changes | Agent reports "success" |
| Requirements met | Line-by-line checklist | Tests passing |

## Red Flags - STOP

- Using "should", "probably", "seems to"
- Expressing satisfaction before verification ("Great!", "Perfect!", "Done!", etc.)
- About to commit/push/PR without verification
- Trusting agent success reports
- Relying on partial verification
- Thinking "just this once"
- Tired and wanting work over
- **ANY wording implying success without having run verification**

## Rationalization Prevention

| Excuse | Reality |
|--------|---------|
| "Should work now" | RUN the verification |
| "I'm confident" | Confidence ≠ evidence |
| "Just this once" | No exceptions |
| "Linter passed" | Linter ≠ compiler |
| "Agent said success" | Verify independently |
| "I'm tired" | Exhaustion ≠ excuse |
| "Partial check is enough" | Partial proves nothing |
| "Different words so rule doesn't apply" | Spirit over letter |

## Key Patterns

**Tests:**
```
✅ [Run test command] [See: 34/34 pass] "All tests pass"
❌ "Should pass now" / "Looks correct"
```

**Regression tests (TDD Red-Green):**
```
✅ Write → Run (pass) → Revert fix → Run (MUST FAIL) → Restore → Run (pass)
❌ "I've written a regression test" (without red-green verification)
```

**Build:**
```
✅ [Run build] [See: exit 0] "Build passes"
❌ "Linter passed" (linter doesn't check compilation)
```

**Requirements:**
```
✅ Re-read plan → Create checklist → Verify each → Report gaps or completion
❌ "Tests pass, phase complete"
```

**Agent delegation:**
```
✅ Agent reports success → Check VCS diff → Verify changes → Report actual state
❌ Trust agent report
```

## Why This Matters

From 24 failure memories:
- your human partner said "I don't believe you" - trust broken
- Undefined functions shipped - would crash
- Missing requirements shipped - incomplete features
- Time wasted on false completion → redirect → rework
- Violates: "Honesty is a core value. If you lie, you'll be replaced."

## When To Apply

**ALWAYS before:**
- ANY variation of success/completion claims
- ANY expression of satisfaction
- ANY positive statement about work state
- Committing, PR creation, task completion
- Moving to next task
- Delegating to agents

**Rule applies to:**
- Exact phrases
- Paraphrases and synonyms
- Implications of success
- ANY communication suggesting completion/correctness

## The Bottom Line

**No shortcuts for verification.**

Run the command. Read the output. THEN claim the result.

This is non-negotiable.

<skill_content name="test-driven-development">
# Skill: test-driven-development

# Test-Driven Development (TDD)

## Overview

Write the test first. Watch it fail. Write minimal code to pass.

**Core principle:** If you didn't watch the test fail, you don't know if it tests the right thing.

**Violating the letter of the rules is violating the spirit of the rules.**

## When to Use

**Always:**
- New features
- Bug fixes
- Refactoring
- Behavior changes

**Exceptions (ask your human partner):**
- Throwaway prototypes
- Generated code
- Configuration files

Thinking "skip TDD just this once"? Stop. That's rationalization.

## The Iron Law

```
NO PRODUCTION CODE WITHOUT A FAILING TEST FIRST
```

Write code before the test? Delete it. Start over.

**No exceptions:**
- Don't keep it as "reference"
- Don't "adapt" it while writing tests
- Don't look at it
- Delete means delete

Implement fresh from tests. Period.

## Red-Green-Refactor

```dot
digraph tdd_cycle {
    rankdir=LR;
    red [label="RED\nWrite failing test", shape=box, style=filled, fillcolor="#ffcccc"];
    verify_red [label="Verify fails\ncorrectly", shape=diamond];
    green [label="GREEN\nMinimal code", shape=box, style=filled, fillcolor="#ccffcc"];
    verify_green [label="Verify passes\nAll green", shape=diamond];
    refactor [label="REFACTOR\nClean up", shape=box, style=filled, fillcolor="#ccccff"];
    next [label="Next", shape=ellipse];

    red -> verify_red;
    verify_red -> green [label="yes"];
    verify_red -> red [label="wrong\nfailure"];
    green -> verify_green;
    verify_green -> refactor [label="yes"];
    verify_green -> green [label="no"];
    refactor -> verify_green [label="stay\ngreen"];
    verify_green -> next;
    next -> red;
}
```

### RED - Write Failing Test

Write one minimal test showing what should happen.

<Good>
```typescript
test('retries failed operations 3 times', async () => {
  let attempts = 0;
  const operation = () => {
    attempts++;
    if (attempts < 3) throw new Error('fail');
    return 'success';
  };

  const result = await retryOperation(operation);

  expect(result).toBe('success');
  expect(attempts).toBe(3);
});
```
Clear name, tests real behavior, one thing
</Good>

<Bad>
```typescript
test('retry works', async () => {
  const mock = jest.fn()
    .mockRejectedValueOnce(new Error())
    .mockRejectedValueOnce(new Error())
    .mockResolvedValueOnce('success');
  await retryOperation(mock);
  expect(mock).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(3);
});
```
Vague name, tests mock not code
</Bad>

**Requirements:**
- One behavior
- Clear name
- Real code (no mocks unless unavoidable)

### Verify RED - Watch It Fail

**MANDATORY. Never skip.**

```bash
npm test path/to/test.test.ts
```

Confirm:
- Test fails (not errors)
- Failure message is expected
- Fails because feature missing (not typos)

**Test passes?** You're testing existing behavior. Fix test.

**Test errors?** Fix error, re-run until it fails correctly.

### GREEN - Minimal Code

Write simplest code to pass the test.

<Good>
```typescript
async function retryOperation<T>(fn: () => Promise<T>): Promise<T> {
  for (let i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
    try {
      return await fn();
    } catch (e) {
      if (i === 2) throw e;
    }
  }
  throw new Error('unreachable');
}
```
Just enough to pass
</Good>

<Bad>
```typescript
async function retryOperation<T>(
  fn: () => Promise<T>,
  options?: {
    maxRetries?: number;
    backoff?: 'linear' | 'exponential';
    onRetry?: (attempt: number) => void;
  }
): Promise<T> {
  // YAGNI
}
```
Over-engineered
</Bad>

Don't add features, refactor other code, or "improve" beyond the test.

### Verify GREEN - Watch It Pass

**MANDATORY.**

```bash
npm test path/to/test.test.ts
```

Confirm:
- Test passes
- Other tests still pass
- Output pristine (no errors, warnings)

**Test fails?** Fix code, not test.

**Other tests fail?** Fix now.

### REFACTOR - Clean Up

After green only:
- Remove duplication
- Improve names
- Extract helpers

Keep tests green. Don't add behavior.

### Repeat

Next failing test for next feature.

## Good Tests

| Quality | Good | Bad |
|---------|------|-----|
| **Minimal** | One thing. "and" in name? Split it. | `test('validates email and domain and whitespace')` |
| **Clear** | Name describes behavior | `test('test1')` |
| **Shows intent** | Demonstrates desired API | Obscures what code should do |

## Why Order Matters

**"I'll write tests after to verify it works"**

Tests written after code pass immediately. Passing immediately proves nothing:
- Might test wrong thing
- Might test implementation, not behavior
- Might miss edge cases you forgot
- You never saw it catch the bug

Test-first forces you to see the test fail, proving it actually tests something.

**"I already manually tested all the edge cases"**

Manual testing is ad-hoc. You think you tested everything but:
- No record of what you tested
- Can't re-run when code changes
- Easy to forget cases under pressure
- "It worked when I tried it" ≠ comprehensive

Automated tests are systematic. They run the same way every time.

**"Deleting X hours of work is wasteful"**

Sunk cost fallacy. The time is already gone. Your choice now:
- Delete and rewrite with TDD (X more hours, high confidence)
- Keep it and add tests after (30 min, low confidence, likely bugs)

The "waste" is keeping code you can't trust. Working code without real tests is technical debt.

**"TDD is dogmatic, being pragmatic means adapting"**

TDD IS pragmatic:
- Finds bugs before commit (faster than debugging after)
- Prevents regressions (tests catch breaks immediately)
- Documents behavior (tests show how to use code)
- Enables refactoring (change freely, tests catch breaks)

"Pragmatic" shortcuts = debugging in production = slower.

**"Tests after achieve the same goals - it's spirit not ritual"**

No. Tests-after answer "What does this do?" Tests-first answer "What should this do?"

Tests-after are biased by your implementation. You test what you built, not what's required. You verify remembered edge cases, not discovered ones.

Tests-first force edge case discovery before implementing. Tests-after verify you remembered everything (you didn't).

30 minutes of tests after ≠ TDD. You get coverage, lose proof tests work.

## Common Rationalizations

| Excuse | Reality |
|--------|---------|
| "Too simple to test" | Simple code breaks. Test takes 30 seconds. |
| "I'll test after" | Tests passing immediately prove nothing. |
| "Tests after achieve same goals" | Tests-after = "what does this do?" Tests-first = "what should this do?" |
| "Already manually tested" | Ad-hoc ≠ systematic. No record, can't re-run. |
| "Deleting X hours is wasteful" | Sunk cost fallacy. Keeping unverified code is technical debt. |
| "Keep as reference, write tests first" | You'll adapt it. That's testing after. Delete means delete. |
| "Need to explore first" | Fine. Throw away exploration, start with TDD. |
| "Test hard = design unclear" | Listen to test. Hard to test = hard to use. |
| "TDD will slow me down" | TDD faster than debugging. Pragmatic = test-first. |
| "Manual test faster" | Manual doesn't prove edge cases. You'll re-test every change. |
| "Existing code has no tests" | You're improving it. Add tests for existing code. |

## Red Flags - STOP and Start Over

- Code before test
- Test after implementation
- Test passes immediately
- Can't explain why test failed
- Tests added "later"
- Rationalizing "just this once"
- "I already manually tested it"
- "Tests after achieve the same purpose"
- "It's about spirit not ritual"
- "Keep as reference" or "adapt existing code"
- "Already spent X hours, deleting is wasteful"
- "TDD is dogmatic, I'm being pragmatic"
- "This is different because..."

**All of these mean: Delete code. Start over with TDD.**

## Example: Bug Fix

**Bug:** Empty email accepted

**RED**
```typescript
test('rejects empty email', async () => {
  const result = await submitForm({ email: '' });
  expect(result.error).toBe('Email required');
});
```

**Verify RED**
```bash
$ npm test
FAIL: expected 'Email required', got undefined
```

**GREEN**
```typescript
function submitForm(data: FormData) {
  if (!data.email?.trim()) {
    return { error: 'Email required' };
  }
  // ...
}
```

**Verify GREEN**
```bash
$ npm test
PASS
```

**REFACTOR**
Extract validation for multiple fields if needed.

## Verification Checklist

Before marking work complete:

- [ ] Every new function/method has a test
- [ ] Watched each test fail before implementing
- [ ] Each test failed for expected reason (feature missing, not typo)
- [ ] Wrote minimal code to pass each test
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Output pristine (no errors, warnings)
- [ ] Tests use real code (mocks only if unavoidable)
- [ ] Edge cases and errors covered

Can't check all boxes? You skipped TDD. Start over.

## When Stuck

| Problem | Solution |
|---------|----------|
| Don't know how to test | Write wished-for API. Write assertion first. Ask your human partner. |
| Test too complicated | Design too complicated. Simplify interface. |
| Must mock everything | Code too coupled. Use dependency injection. |
| Test setup huge | Extract helpers. Still complex? Simplify design. |

## Debugging Integration

Bug found? Write failing test reproducing it. Follow TDD cycle. Test proves fix and prevents regression.

Never fix bugs without a test.

## Testing Anti-Patterns

When adding mocks or test utilities, read @testing-anti-patterns.md to avoid common pitfalls:
- Testing mock behavior instead of real behavior
- Adding test-only methods to production classes
- Mocking without understanding dependencies

## Final Rule

```
Production code → test exists and failed first
Otherwise → not TDD
```

No exceptions without your human partner's permission.

<content>1: // ─── Client Init (Ticket 3.6) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // Handles the initial handshake when a browser client connects via WebSocket.
3: // Sends session info (with cached events or REST API history), model info,
4: // agent list, provider/model list, and PTY replay to the new client.
5: //
6: // Extracted from relay-stack.ts's `client_connected` handler so the logic is
7: // independently testable and relay-stack stays slim.
8: 
9: import { formatErrorDetail, RelayError } from "./errors.js";
10: import { filterAgents } from "./handlers/index.js";
11: import type { MessageCache } from "./message-cache.js";
12: import type { OpenCodeClient } from "./opencode-client.js";
13: import type { PtyManager } from "./pty-manager.js";
14: import type { SessionManager } from "./session-manager.js";
15: import type { SessionOverrides } from "./session-overrides.js";
16: import type { RelayMessage } from "./types.js";
17: 
18: // ─── Dependencies ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
19: 
20: export interface ClientInitDeps {
21: 	wsHandler: {
22: 		broadcast: (msg: RelayMessage) => void;
23: 		sendTo: (clientId: string, msg: RelayMessage) => void;
24: 	};
25: 	client: OpenCodeClient;
26: 	sessionMgr: SessionManager;
27: 	messageCache: MessageCache;
28: 	overrides: SessionOverrides;
29: 	ptyManager: PtyManager;
30: 	log: (...args: unknown[]) => void;
31: }
32: 
33: // ─── Handler ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
34: 
35: /**
36:  * Handle a newly connected browser client. Sends all initial state:
37:  * - Active session with cached events or REST API history
38:  * - Session list
39:  * - Model info (from session or overrides)
40:  * - Agent list (filtered)
41:  * - Provider/model list (connected only)
42:  * - PTY list + scrollback replay
43:  *
44:  * Errors are sent as INIT_FAILED messages without crashing the handler.
45:  */
46: export async function handleClientConnected(
47: 	deps: ClientInitDeps,
48: 	clientId: string,
49: ): Promise<void> {
50: 	const { wsHandler, client, sessionMgr, messageCache, overrides, ptyManager } =
51: 		deps;
52: 
53: 	const sendInitError = (err: unknown, prefix: string) => {
54: 		deps.log(`   [ws] ${prefix}: ${formatErrorDetail(err)}`);
55: 		wsHandler.sendTo(
56: 			clientId,
57: 			RelayError.fromCaught(err, "INIT_FAILED", prefix).toMessage(),
58: 		);
59: 	};
60: 
61: 	// ── Active session with event replay ─────────────────────────────────
62: 	const activeId = sessionMgr.getActiveSessionId();
63: 	if (activeId) {
64: 		const events = messageCache.getEvents(activeId);
65: 		const hasChatContent =
66: 			events?.some((e) => e.type === "user_message" || e.type === "delta") ??
67: 			false;
68: 		if (events && hasChatContent) {
69: 			// Cache hit with content: include raw events for client replay
70: 			wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
71: 				type: "session_switched",
72: 				id: activeId,
73: 				events,
74: 			});
75: 		} else {
76: 			// Cache miss (session from before relay started): REST API fallback
77: 			try {
78: 				const history = await sessionMgr.loadHistory(activeId);
79: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
80: 					type: "session_switched",
81: 					id: activeId,
82: 					history: {
83: 						messages: history.messages,
84: 						hasMore: history.hasMore,
85: 						total: history.total,
86: 					},
87: 				});
88: 			} catch {
89: 				// If REST API fails too, just send the switch without history
90: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
91: 					type: "session_switched",
92: 					id: activeId,
93: 				});
94: 			}
95: 		}
96: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, { type: "status", status: "idle" });
97: 
98: 		// Send model/agent info from the active session
99: 		try {
100: 			const session = await client.getSession(activeId);
101: 			if (session.modelID) {
102: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
103: 					type: "model_info",
104: 					model: session.modelID,
105: 					provider: session.providerID ?? "",
106: 				});
107: 			} else if (overrides.model) {
108: 				// Session has no model set, but relay has a selected model
109: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
110: 					type: "model_info",
111: 					model: overrides.model.modelID,
112: 					provider: overrides.model.providerID,
113: 				});
114: 			}
115: 		} catch (err) {
116: 			sendInitError(err, "Failed to load session info");
117: 			if (overrides.model) {
118: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
119: 					type: "model_info",
120: 					model: overrides.model.modelID,
121: 					provider: overrides.model.providerID,
122: 				});
123: 			}
124: 		}
125: 	}
126: 
127: 	// ── Session list ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
128: 	try {
129: 		const sessions = await sessionMgr.listSessions();
130: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, { type: "session_list", sessions });
131: 	} catch (err) {
132: 		sendInitError(err, "Failed to list sessions");
133: 	}
134: 
135: 	// ── Agent list (filter out internal agents) ──────────────────────────
136: 	try {
137: 		const rawAgents = await client.listAgents();
138: 		const agents = filterAgents(rawAgents);
139: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, { type: "agent_list", agents });
140: 	} catch (err) {
141: 		sendInitError(err, "Failed to list agents");
142: 	}
143: 
144: 	// ── Provider/model list + auto-select default ────────────────────────
145: 	try {
146: 		const providerResult = await client.listProviders();
147: 		const connectedSet = new Set(providerResult.connected);
148: 		const providers = providerResult.providers
149: 			.map((p) => ({
150: 				id: p.id || p.name || "",
151: 				name: p.name || p.id || "",
152: 				configured: connectedSet.has(p.id) || connectedSet.has(p.name),
153: 				models: (p.models ?? []).map((m) => ({
154: 					id: m.id,
155: 					name: m.name || m.id,
156: 					provider: p.id || p.name || "",
157: 				})),
158: 			}))
159: 			.filter((p) => p.configured);
160: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, { type: "model_list", providers });
161: 
162: 		// Auto-select default model from a connected provider if none set
163: 		if (!overrides.model) {
164: 			for (const providerId of providerResult.connected) {
165: 				const defaultModelId = providerResult.defaults[providerId];
166: 				if (defaultModelId) {
167: 					overrides.setModelDefault({
168: 						providerID: providerId,
169: 						modelID: defaultModelId,
170: 					});
171: 					wsHandler.broadcast({
172: 						type: "model_info",
173: 						model: defaultModelId,
174: 						provider: providerId,
175: 					});
176: 					deps.log(
177: 						`   [model] Auto-selected default: ${defaultModelId} (${providerId})`,
178: 					);
179: 					break;
180: 				}
181: 			}
182: 		}
183: 	} catch (err) {
184: 		sendInitError(err, "Failed to list providers");
185: 	}
186: 
187: 	// ── PTY list + scrollback replay ─────────────────────────────────────
188: 	if (ptyManager.sessionCount > 0) {
189: 		const ptys = ptyManager.listSessions();
190: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, { type: "pty_list", ptys });
191: 		// Replay scrollback for each PTY to this specific client
192: 		for (const { id: ptyId } of ptys) {
193: 			const scrollback = ptyManager.getScrollback(ptyId);
194: 			if (scrollback) {
195: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
196: 					type: "pty_output",
197: 					ptyId,
198: 					data: scrollback,
199: 				});
200: 			}
201: 			const session = ptyManager.getSession(ptyId);
202: 			if (session?.exited) {
203: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
204: 					type: "pty_exited",
205: 					ptyId,
206: 					exitCode: session.exitCode ?? 0,
207: 				});
208: 			}
209: 		}
210: 	}
211: }

<content>1: // ─── Permission Bridge (Ticket 1.5) ─────────────────────────────────────────
2: 
3: import type {
4: 	FrontendDecision,
5: 	OpenCodeDecision,
6: 	OpenCodeEvent,
7: 	PendingPermission,
8: } from "./types.js";
9: 
10: // ─── Decision Mapping ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
11: 
12: const DECISION_MAP: Record<FrontendDecision, OpenCodeDecision> = {
13: 	allow: "once",
14: 	deny: "reject",
15: 	allow_always: "always",
16: };
17: 
18: const REVERSE_DECISION_MAP: Record<OpenCodeDecision, FrontendDecision> = {
19: 	once: "allow",
20: 	reject: "deny",
21: 	always: "allow_always",
22: };
23: 
24: /** Map frontend decision vocabulary → OpenCode vocabulary */
25: export function mapDecision(frontend: string): OpenCodeDecision | null {
26: 	return DECISION_MAP[frontend as FrontendDecision] ?? null;
27: }
28: 
29: /** Map OpenCode decision vocabulary → frontend vocabulary */
30: export function mapDecisionReverse(opencode: string): FrontendDecision | null {
31: 	return REVERSE_DECISION_MAP[opencode as OpenCodeDecision] ?? null;
32: }
33: 
34: // ─── Permission Bridge ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
35: 
36: export interface PermissionBridgeOptions {
37: 	timeoutMs?: number;
38: 	now?: () => number;
39: }
40: 
41: export class PermissionBridge {
42: 	private pending: Map<string, PendingPermission> = new Map();
43: 	private alwaysAllowed: Set<string> = new Set();
44: 	private readonly timeoutMs: number;
45: 	private readonly now: () => number;
46: 
47: 	constructor(options: PermissionBridgeOptions = {}) {
48: 		this.timeoutMs = options.timeoutMs ?? 5 * 60_000; // 5 minutes
49: 		this.now = options.now ?? Date.now;
50: 	}
51: 
52: 	/** Process an incoming permission.asked SSE event */
53: 	onPermissionRequest(event: OpenCodeEvent): PendingPermission | null {
54: 		const props = event.properties as {
55: 			id?: string;
56: 			permission?: string;
57: 			patterns?: string[];
58: 			metadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
59: 			always?: string[];
60: 		};
61: 
62: 		if (!props.id || !props.permission) return null;
63: 
64: 		// Auto-approve if tool is in the always-allowed set
65: 		if (this.alwaysAllowed.has(props.permission)) {
66: 			return null; // Caller should auto-reply
67: 		}
68: 
69: 		const entry: PendingPermission = {
70: 			requestId: props.id,
71: 			toolName: props.permission,
72: 			toolInput: {
73: 				patterns: props.patterns ?? [],
74: 				metadata: props.metadata ?? {},
75: 			},
76: 			always: props.always ?? [],
77: 			timestamp: this.now(),
78: 		};
79: 
80: 		this.pending.set(props.id, entry);
81: 		return entry;
82: 	}
83: 
84: 	/** Process a user's permission decision from the browser */
85: 	onPermissionResponse(
86: 		requestId: string,
87: 		decision: string,
88: 	): { mapped: OpenCodeDecision; toolName: string } | null {
89: 		const entry = this.pending.get(requestId);
90: 		if (!entry) return null; // Already resolved or unknown — ignore duplicate
91: 
92: 		const mapped = mapDecision(decision);
93: 		if (!mapped) return null;
94: 
95: 		// Track "always" decisions
96: 		if (mapped === "always") {
97: 			this.alwaysAllowed.add(entry.toolName);
98: 		}
99: 
100: 		this.pending.delete(requestId);
101: 		return { mapped, toolName: entry.toolName };
102: 	}
103: 
104: 	/** Handle a permission.replied SSE event (resolved from CLI) */
105: 	onPermissionReplied(requestId: string): boolean {
106: 		return this.pending.delete(requestId);
107: 	}
108: 
109: 	/** Get all pending permissions (for replay on reconnect) */
110: 	getPending(): PendingPermission[] {
111: 		return Array.from(this.pending.values());
112: 	}
113: 
114: 	/** Check if a tool is in the always-allowed set */
115: 	isAlwaysAllowed(toolName: string): boolean {
116: 		return this.alwaysAllowed.has(toolName);
117: 	}
118: 
119: 	/** Clear the always-allowed cache */
120: 	clearAlwaysAllowed(): void {
121: 		this.alwaysAllowed.clear();
122: 	}
123: 
124: 	/** Check for timed-out permissions and return their IDs */
125: 	checkTimeouts(): string[] {
126: 		const now = this.now();
127: 		const timedOut: string[] = [];
128: 
129: 		for (const [id, entry] of this.pending) {
130: 			if (now - entry.timestamp >= this.timeoutMs) {
131: 				timedOut.push(id);
132: 				this.pending.delete(id);
133: 			}
134: 		}
135: 
136: 		return timedOut;
137: 	}
138: 
139: 	/** Recover pending permissions from REST API response */
140: 	recoverPending(
141: 		permissions: Array<{
142: 			id: string;
143: 			permission: string;
144: 			patterns?: string[];
145: 			metadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
146: 			always?: string[];
147: 		}>,
148: 	): PendingPermission[] {

157: 				},

160: 			};
161: 			this.pending.set(p.id, entry);
162: 			recovered.push(entry);
163: 		}

<content>1: // ─── Question / Ask-User Bridge (Ticket 1.6) ────────────────────────────────
2: 
3: import type { AskUserQuestion, OpenCodeEvent } from "./types.js";
4: 
5: export interface PendingQuestion {
6: 	toolId: string;
7: 	questions: AskUserQuestion[];
8: 	timestamp: number;
9: }
10: 
11: export interface QuestionBridgeOptions {
12: 	timeoutMs?: number;
13: 	now?: () => number;
14: }
15: 
16: /**
17:  * Convert browser answer format to OpenCode's format.
18:  * Browser: { "0": "Option A", "1": "Option B" }
19:  * OpenCode: [["Option A"], ["Option B"]]
20:  *
21:  * For multi-select:
22:  * Browser: { "0": "Opt A, Opt B" }
23:  * OpenCode: [["Opt A", "Opt B"]]
24:  */
25: export function convertAnswers(
26: 	answers: Record<string, string>,
27: 	questions: AskUserQuestion[],
28: ): string[][] {
29: 	const result: string[][] = [];
30: 
31: 	for (let i = 0; i < questions.length; i++) {
32: 		const key = String(i);
33: 		const raw = answers[key];
34: 
35: 		if (raw === undefined || raw === "") {
36: 			result.push([]);
37: 			continue;
38: 		}
39: 
40: 		if (questions[i]!.multiSelect) {
41: 			// Split comma-separated selections
42: 			result.push(
43: 				raw
44: 					.split(",")
45: 					.map((s) => s.trim())
46: 					.filter((s) => s.length > 0),
47: 			);
48: 		} else {
49: 			result.push([raw]);
50: 		}
51: 	}
52: 
53: 	return result;
54: }
55: 
56: /**
57:  * Map OpenCode question format (with `multiple` field) to frontend format (with `multiSelect` field).
58:  */
59: export function mapQuestionFields(
60: 	ocQuestions: Array<{
61: 		question?: string;
62: 		header?: string;
63: 		options?: Array<{ label?: string; description?: string }>;
64: 		multiple?: boolean;
65: 		custom?: boolean;
66: 	}>,
67: ): AskUserQuestion[] {
68: 	return ocQuestions.map((q) => ({
69: 		question: q.question ?? "",
70: 		header: q.header ?? "",
71: 		options: (q.options ?? []).map((o) => ({
72: 			label: o.label ?? "",
73: 			description: o.description ?? "",
74: 		})),
75: 		multiSelect: q.multiple ?? false,
76: 		custom: q.custom ?? true,
77: 	}));
78: }
79: 
80: export class QuestionBridge {
81: 	private pending: Map<string, PendingQuestion> = new Map();
82: 	private readonly timeoutMs: number;
83: 	private readonly now: () => number;
84: 
85: 	constructor(options: QuestionBridgeOptions = {}) {
86: 		this.timeoutMs = options.timeoutMs ?? 10 * 60_000; // 10 minutes
87: 		this.now = options.now ?? Date.now;
88: 	}
89: 
90: 	/** Process an incoming question.asked SSE event */
91: 	onQuestion(event: OpenCodeEvent): PendingQuestion | null {
92: 		const props = event.properties as {
93: 			id?: string;
94: 			questions?: Array<{
95: 				question?: string;
96: 				header?: string;
97: 				options?: Array<{ label?: string; description?: string }>;
98: 				multiple?: boolean;
99: 				custom?: boolean;
100: 			}>;
101: 		};
102: 
103: 		if (!props.id || !props.questions) return null;
104: 
105: 		const questions = mapQuestionFields(props.questions);
106: 		const entry: PendingQuestion = {
107: 			toolId: props.id,
108: 			questions,
109: 			timestamp: this.now(),
110: 		};
111: 
112: 		this.pending.set(props.id, entry);
113: 		return entry;
114: 	}
115: 
116: 	/** Process a user's answer from the browser */
117: 	onAnswer(
118: 		toolId: string,
119: 		answers: Record<string, string>,
120: 	): { formatted: string[][] } | null {
121: 		const entry = this.pending.get(toolId);
122: 		if (!entry) return null;
123: 
124: 		const formatted = convertAnswers(answers, entry.questions);
125: 		this.pending.delete(toolId);
126: 		return { formatted };
127: 	}
128: 
129: 	/** Get all pending questions (for replay on reconnect) */
130: 	getPending(): PendingQuestion[] {
131: 		return Array.from(this.pending.values());
132: 	}
133: 
134: 	/** Check for timed-out questions */
135: 	checkTimeouts(): string[] {
136: 		const now = this.now();
137: 		const timedOut: string[] = [];
138: 
139: 		for (const [id, entry] of this.pending) {
140: 			if (now - entry.timestamp >= this.timeoutMs) {
141: 				timedOut.push(id);
142: 				// NOTE: per spec, questions do NOT auto-answer on timeout
143: 				// but we track them for notification purposes
144: 			}
145: 		}
146: 
147: 		return timedOut;
148: 	}
149: 
150: 	/** Remove a question (e.g., after timeout notification) */
151: 	remove(toolId: string): boolean {
152: 		return this.pending.delete(toolId);
153: 	}
154: 
155: 	get size(): number {
156: 		return this.pending.size;
157: 	}
158: }

<content>1: // ─── Client Init (Ticket 3.6) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // Handles the initial handshake when a browser client connects via WebSocket.
3: // Sends session info (with cached events or REST API history), model info,
4: // agent list, provider/model list, and PTY replay to the new client.
5: //
6: // Extracted from relay-stack.ts's `client_connected` handler so the logic is
7: // independently testable and relay-stack stays slim.
8: 
9: import { formatErrorDetail, RelayError } from "./errors.js";
10: import { filterAgents } from "./handlers/index.js";
11: import type { MessageCache } from "./message-cache.js";
12: import type { OpenCodeClient } from "./opencode-client.js";
13: import type { PermissionBridge } from "./permission-bridge.js";
14: import type { PtyManager } from "./pty-manager.js";
15: import type { QuestionBridge } from "./question-bridge.js";
16: import type { SessionManager } from "./session-manager.js";
17: import type { SessionOverrides } from "./session-overrides.js";
18: import type { RelayMessage } from "./types.js";
19: 
20: // ─── Dependencies ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
21: 
22: export interface ClientInitDeps {
23: 	wsHandler: {
24: 		broadcast: (msg: RelayMessage) => void;
25: 		sendTo: (clientId: string, msg: RelayMessage) => void;
26: 	};
27: 	client: OpenCodeClient;
28: 	sessionMgr: SessionManager;
29: 	messageCache: MessageCache;
30: 	overrides: SessionOverrides;
31: 	ptyManager: PtyManager;
32: 	permissionBridge: Pick<PermissionBridge, "getPending">;
33: 	questionBridge: Pick<QuestionBridge, "getPending">;
34: 	log: (...args: unknown[]) => void;
35: }
36: 
37: // ─── Handler ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
38: 
39: /**
40:  * Handle a newly connected browser client. Sends all initial state:
41:  * - Active session with cached events or REST API history
42:  * - Session list
43:  * - Model info (from session or overrides)
44:  * - Agent list (filtered)
45:  * - Provider/model list (connected only)
46:  * - PTY list + scrollback replay
47:  *
48:  * Errors are sent as INIT_FAILED messages without crashing the handler.
49:  */
50: export async function handleClientConnected(
51: 	deps: ClientInitDeps,
52: 	clientId: string,
53: ): Promise<void> {
54: 	const {
55: 		wsHandler,
56: 		client,
57: 		sessionMgr,
58: 		messageCache,
59: 		overrides,
60: 		ptyManager,
61: 		permissionBridge,
62: 		questionBridge,
63: 	} = deps;
64: 
65: 	const sendInitError = (err: unknown, prefix: string) => {
66: 		deps.log(`   [ws] ${prefix}: ${formatErrorDetail(err)}`);
67: 		wsHandler.sendTo(
68: 			clientId,
69: 			RelayError.fromCaught(err, "INIT_FAILED", prefix).toMessage(),
70: 		);
71: 	};
72: 
73: 	// ── Active session with event replay ─────────────────────────────────
74: 	const activeId = sessionMgr.getActiveSessionId();
75: 	if (activeId) {
76: 		const events = messageCache.getEvents(activeId);
77: 		const hasChatContent =
78: 			events?.some((e) => e.type === "user_message" || e.type === "delta") ??
79: 			false;
80: 		if (events && hasChatContent) {
81: 			// Cache hit with content: include raw events for client replay
82: 			wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
83: 				type: "session_switched",
84: 				id: activeId,
85: 				events,
86: 			});
87: 		} else {
88: 			// Cache miss (session from before relay started): REST API fallback
89: 			try {
90: 				const history = await sessionMgr.loadHistory(activeId);
91: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
92: 					type: "session_switched",
93: 					id: activeId,
94: 					history: {
95: 						messages: history.messages,
96: 						hasMore: history.hasMore,
97: 						total: history.total,
98: 					},
99: 				});
100: 			} catch {
101: 				// If REST API fails too, just send the switch without history
102: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
103: 					type: "session_switched",
104: 					id: activeId,
105: 				});
106: 			}
107: 		}
108: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, { type: "status", status: "idle" });
109: 
110: 		// Send model/agent info from the active session
111: 		try {
112: 			const session = await client.getSession(activeId);
113: 			if (session.modelID) {
114: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
115: 					type: "model_info",
116: 					model: session.modelID,
117: 					provider: session.providerID ?? "",
118: 				});
119: 			} else if (overrides.model) {
120: 				// Session has no model set, but relay has a selected model
121: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
122: 					type: "model_info",
123: 					model: overrides.model.modelID,
124: 					provider: overrides.model.providerID,
125: 				});
126: 			}
127: 		} catch (err) {
128: 			sendInitError(err, "Failed to load session info");
129: 			if (overrides.model) {
130: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
131: 					type: "model_info",
132: 					model: overrides.model.modelID,
133: 					provider: overrides.model.providerID,
134: 				});
135: 			}
136: 		}
137: 	}
138: 
139: 	// ── Session list ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
140: 	try {
141: 		const sessions = await sessionMgr.listSessions();
142: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, { type: "session_list", sessions });
143: 	} catch (err) {
144: 		sendInitError(err, "Failed to list sessions");
145: 	}
146: 
147: 	// ── Pending permissions + questions (reconnect replay) ───────────────
148: 	for (const perm of permissionBridge.getPending()) {
149: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
150: 			type: "permission_request",
151: 			requestId: perm.requestId,
152: 			toolName: perm.toolName,
153: 			toolInput: perm.toolInput,
154: 		});
155: 	}
156: 	for (const q of questionBridge.getPending()) {
157: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
158: 			type: "ask_user",
159: 			toolId: q.toolId,
160: 			questions: q.questions,
161: 		});
162: 	}
163: 
164: 	// ── Agent list (filter out internal agents) ──────────────────────────
165: 	try {
166: 		const rawAgents = await client.listAgents();
167: 		const agents = filterAgents(rawAgents);
168: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, { type: "agent_list", agents });
169: 	} catch (err) {
170: 		sendInitError(err, "Failed to list agents");
171: 	}
172: 
173: 	// ── Provider/model list + auto-select default ────────────────────────
174: 	try {
175: 		const providerResult = await client.listProviders();
176: 		const connectedSet = new Set(providerResult.connected);
177: 		const providers = providerResult.providers
178: 			.map((p) => ({
179: 				id: p.id || p.name || "",
180: 				name: p.name || p.id || "",
181: 				configured: connectedSet.has(p.id) || connectedSet.has(p.name),
182: 				models: (p.models ?? []).map((m) => ({
183: 					id: m.id,
184: 					name: m.name || m.id,
185: 					provider: p.id || p.name || "",
186: 				})),
187: 			}))
188: 			.filter((p) => p.configured);
189: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, { type: "model_list", providers });
190: 
191: 		// Auto-select default model from a connected provider if none set
192: 		if (!overrides.model) {
193: 			for (const providerId of providerResult.connected) {
194: 				const defaultModelId = providerResult.defaults[providerId];
195: 				if (defaultModelId) {
196: 					overrides.setModelDefault({
197: 						providerID: providerId,
198: 						modelID: defaultModelId,
199: 					});
200: 					wsHandler.broadcast({
201: 						type: "model_info",
202: 						model: defaultModelId,
203: 						provider: providerId,
204: 					});
205: 					deps.log(
206: 						`   [model] Auto-selected default: ${defaultModelId} (${providerId})`,
207: 					);
208: 					break;
209: 				}
210: 			}
211: 		}
212: 	} catch (err) {
213: 		sendInitError(err, "Failed to list providers");
214: 	}
215: 
216: 	// ── PTY list + scrollback replay ─────────────────────────────────────
217: 	if (ptyManager.sessionCount > 0) {
218: 		const ptys = ptyManager.listSessions();
219: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, { type: "pty_list", ptys });
220: 		// Replay scrollback for each PTY to this specific client
221: 		for (const { id: ptyId } of ptys) {
222: 			const scrollback = ptyManager.getScrollback(ptyId);
223: 			if (scrollback) {
224: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
225: 					type: "pty_output",
226: 					ptyId,
227: 					data: scrollback,
228: 				});
229: 			}
230: 			const session = ptyManager.getSession(ptyId);
231: 			if (session?.exited) {
232: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
233: 					type: "pty_exited",
234: 					ptyId,
235: 					exitCode: session.exitCode ?? 0,
236: 				});
237: 			}
238: 		}
239: 	}
240: }
